description: Bromeliaceae contains ~75 genera and 3500+ species. Native almost entirely to the Americas (except one African Pitcairnia). Includes the commercially important pineapple (Ananas comosus) and hundreds of popular ornamental air plants (Tillandsia). India cultivates pineapple extensively in Kerala and Northeast states.
meta_title: Bromeliaceae Taxonomy Pack — Tillandsia, Neoregelia, Aechmea, Guzmania
meta_description: Comprehensive bromeliad species list: air plants, tank bromeliads, earth stars, pineapple.
meta_keywords: bromeliad, Tillandsia, air plant, Neoregelia, Aechmea, Vriesea, Guzmania, pineapple

genera.description: 250+ species of urn bromeliads from Central and South America. Long-lasting inflorescences. Widely grown as houseplants and in tropical landscaping.
35 species of giant terrestrial/epilithic bromeliads from Brazil's rocky outcrops.
5 species including A. comosus, the world's most commercially important bromeliad. India: Ananas, cultivated extensively in Northeast India and Kerala.
60+ species of tube-forming bromeliads from the Americas. B. nutans (Queen's Tears) is the easiest bromeliad to grow.
60+ species of terrestrial bromeliads from Brazil. Flat star-shaped rosettes with beautifully striped leaves. Popular terrarium plants.
200+ species of terrestrial bromeliads from South America. Stiff, spine-edged leaves. Drought-tolerant.
210+ species of epiphytic bromeliads from tropical Americas. Long-lasting bracts in red, orange, yellow, pink. G. lingulata is one of the most widely sold bromeliads.
70+ species of xerophytic bromeliads from Mexico. Dioecious — separate male and female plants.
100+ species of flat, open-rosette bromeliads. Leaves colour brilliantly at flowering. Popular in tropical gardens and as houseplants.
230+ species, the largest bromeliad genus by plant size. Some reach 10m tall. P. raimondii is the world's largest bromeliad — blooms once then dies.
650+ species — the largest bromeliad genus. Most are epiphytes requiring no soil. Extremely popular in terrariums and mounted displays. All CITES Appendix II.
230+ species. Known for flat, sword-like flower spikes. V. splendens (Flaming Sword) is among the most popular indoor bromeliads.
